scores.reasoning: The price of a company’s shares is by definition a crowd forecast of its future value. All available company information  including analysis and expectations of the investors  is quickly and efficiently aggregated and incorporated into the stock price. The past performance of a company’s stock is not a reliable predictor of future performance.   Consequently  it is practically impossible to accurately predict the movement of markets in the short term.   For what concerns the long-term  it is possible to make some basic assumptions about the generally growing trajectory of the US economy  and about the fact that companies aim at being part of such growth. Consequently  one would expect stock prices to be at least moderately higher in the long run.   I took the prices for all of the 2014 SP500 components and checked where they stand today to get a basic sense of what is the base rate chance that companies keep growing over the next 5-10 Years. Then  Im skewing to account for the recent performance of individual companies and for general considerations about the sectors they operate in.   AT&Ts stock price has been going up and down in the past few years but did not show organic growth. [redacted to maintain anonymity] there might be technical reasons why this is the case. The company makes both acquisitions and sells some companies. Im not moving from 50% all the way.
